rule.desc: Circular module dependencies that cause runtime initialization errors or `undefined` imports.
rule.better: . Extract shared types into a separate module. Use `import type` for type-only cycles. Restructure to break the loop.
rule.why_bad: Circular dependencies can produce `undefined` at runtime depending on module resolution order. They indicate poor separation of concerns.

rule.desc: Barrel files (`index.ts`) that re-export everything from every module, causing bundle bloat and slow IDE performance.
rule.better: . Use barrels only at package boundaries. Import directly from specific modules for internal code.
rule.why_bad: Deep barrels force bundlers to load entire dependency trees. They slow down IDE auto-completion and type checking.

rule.desc: Modules that execute side effects on import (register globals, modify prototypes, start servers).
rule.better: Export initialization functions that consumers call explicitly. Keep module top-level code pure.
rule.why_bad: Side-effect imports create hidden coupling, break tree-shaking, make testing difficult, and cause order-dependent bugs.

rule.desc: Using `export * from` which leaks implementation details and creates name collisions.
rule.better: . Use explicit named re-exports to control the public API.
rule.why_bad: Wildcard re-exports expose internal types, cause ambiguous exports when names collide, and make API surface unpredictable.

rule.desc: Default exports that lose their name binding, causing inconsistent names across imports.
rule.better: . Use named exports so the name is consistent everywhere.
rule.why_bad: Default exports can be imported under any name, leading to inconsistent naming across the codebase and harder searching/refactoring.
